emptyLibraryFirstRunHint: First time here? We recommend scanning to automatically find skills you already have.
variantLabelBody: Use a stable label to distinguish same-family variants inside the managed library.
conflictInstallBody: This target already contains different content for the same slug. Review the directory before replacing it.
scanDiskBody: Run a manual full-disk discovery for supported Codex, Claude Code, and OpenClaw skill layouts.
scanConfirmBody: Full-disk discovery can take noticeable time. Skill Manager will scan supported skill layouts, update the local index, and then let you review grouped candidates before adoption.

errorNetwork: Network connection failed. Please check your internet and try again.

errorValidation: The input is invalid. Please check your settings and try again.

errorAlreadyExists: This item already exists. Please remove the existing version first.
errorUnsupported: This action is not supported for the selected item.

importFolderBody: Import a local skill folder directly into the managed library.
remoteSearchBody: Search skills.sh and adopt a selected skill into the managed library.
offlineModeBody: Registry search is unavailable without an internet connection. You can still import skills from your local disk.
remoteNoResults: No registry skills matched this query.
discoveredSkillsBody: These candidates are grouped by skill family so you can review exact duplicates, keep variants, and batch-adopt the right set.
noDiscoveredSkills: Run a disk scan to populate discovery results.
adoptNoteBody: Adopt copies the whole skill directory into ~/.skill-manager/store/. Install target sync comes next.
exactDuplicateSectionBody: These families collapse multiple disk occurrences down to one representative candidate.
variantSectionBody: These families share a name but differ in content. Review the candidate representatives before adoption.
uniqueSectionBody: These families currently have a single candidate and can be adopted directly.
resolutionBody: Review matching managed variants, merge exact duplicates, or create a new named branch for content that should stay separate.
compareEmptyBody: Select a candidate and a managed variant to inspect their SKILL.md and file-level differences.
promoteHint: Only affects future installs. Existing installations stay unchanged.
diffPathNotAllowedError: Cannot compare: this variant is outside the current workspace scope.
noRecordedInstallsBody: No managed installs are recorded for this target yet. Install from Library to start tracking it here.
noTargetsBody: Target health will appear once scan roots or managed install targets are available.
originsEmptyBody: No origin records have been captured for this managed skill yet. Future adoptions will preserve them.
familyVariantsBody: These managed variants share the same family. Switch between them here instead of treating them as unrelated skills.
overviewVersioningBody: This managed family can keep multiple named variants, compare them, and promote one as the default install line.
warningsBody: Non-fatal issues collected while indexing and classifying skills.
savedLanguageHint: Language is persisted locally and applied on the next launch.
cacheStatusBody: Startup reads the local SQLite index first, then refreshes in the background when stale.
installStrategyBody: Managed skills are installed outward by symlink first. Copy mode remains a compatibility fallback for a later settings expansion.
discoveryScopeBody: Current full-disk discovery looks for supported Codex, Claude Code, and OpenClaw install layouts.
emptyDiscoverBody: Run a full-disk scan and Skill Manager will find Codex, Claude Code, and OpenClaw skills on your machine.
emptyTargetsBody: Target health will appear once scan roots or managed install targets are available.
emptyUniqueBody: No unique items ready to adopt.
noMatchingSkillsBody: No skills match the current filters.
noSkillsInLibraryBody: Scan your disk for existing skills or import a local folder.
noDiffAvailable: Diff is not available for this file type.
savedThemeHint: Your preference is saved for the next session.
guideBody: How Skill Manager works and how to get the most out of it.
guideQuickStartBody:
1. Open **Discover** and browse the Marketplace or run a local scan.
2. **Adopt** a skill into your managed library.
3. Go to **Library**, select the skill, and click **Install**.
4. Check **Targets** to verify the install is healthy.

Skill Manager handles versioning, deduplication, and symlink management so your AI agents always have the right skills installed.
guideLibraryBody:
Your managed skill inventory. Key features:

- **Search & filter** — by name, agent type, or scope
- **Gallery view** — each card shows name, description, agent, and scope
- **Detail panel** — view SKILL.md content, file tree, install history, and Git origin
- **Variants** — compare snapshots of the same family and set a default
- **Install** — symlink or copy to any registered target
- **Update** — pull newer versions from the registry when available
guideDiscoverBody:
Two ways to find skills:

**Marketplace** — browse popular skills from skills.sh or search the registry. Click "Add to Library" to adopt.

**Local Discovery** — scan your disk for existing Codex, Claude Code, Agent, and OpenClaw skill folders. Results are grouped by family:
- **Ready** — unique skills, adopt directly
- **Duplicates** — identical copies found in multiple locations
- **Needs review** — same family but different content; use the Review modal to merge or create named variants
guideTargetsBody:
Install targets are directories where skills live on disk. Each target belongs to an agent and scope:

- **Codex** — .codex/ prefix
- **Claude Code** — .claude/ prefix
- **Agent** — .agent/ prefix
- **OpenClaw** — .openclaw/workspace/skills prefix

Scope is either **Global** (user-wide) or **Project** (workspace-local).

Use this page to install, remove, repair, or relink skills. Health indicators: green = healthy, yellow = warning, red = broken or missing.
guideSettingsBody:
Configurable options:

- **Language** — English or Chinese
- **Theme** — Light or Dark
- **Index path** — where the local SQLite index is stored
- **Store path** — where managed skill copies live (~/.skill-manager/store/)
- **Install strategy** — Symlink first (default), with copy as fallback
- **Custom targets** — add arbitrary directories as install locations
guideShortcutsBody:
⌘/Ctrl + 1 — Library
⌘/Ctrl + 2 — Discover
⌘/Ctrl + 3 — Targets
⌘/Ctrl + 4 — Settings
⌘/Ctrl + 5 — Guide
⌘/Ctrl + K — Focus search field
⌘/Ctrl + R — Refresh skill index
← / → — Navigate tabs when focused on tab bar
guideFaqBody:
**Why is my skill not showing up?**
Make sure it has a valid SKILL.md file in the root directory. Run a disk scan from Discover to re-index.

**Can I install the same skill for multiple agents?**
Yes — open the Install dialog and select the target agent and scope.

**What does "Update available" mean?**
The remote registry has a newer version than your local copy. Click Update to pull it.

**How do I fix a broken symlink?**
Go to Targets, find the affected target, and click Repair.

**Where are my skills stored?**
Managed copies live in ~/.skill-manager/store/. Installs are symlinks pointing back to this store.
customTargetsBody: Add arbitrary directories as install targets. Skills can be symlink-installed into any custom target you define here.
noCustomTargets: No custom targets yet. Add one to install skills anywhere.
customTargetDeleteConfirm: Remove this install target? Existing symlinks will not be deleted.

manageCustomTargetsBody: These are directories you have previously installed skills into. You can remove records here; existing symlinks on disk will not be deleted.
